spec_match.rationale: The active stylesheet now declares --si: sibling-index() and --sc: sibling-count() on li.track, and each row computes the hue from those CSS tree-counting functions.

interactive.rationale: Project presets plus add/remove controls still rebuild the sibling list, causing the CSS-computed hue distribution to rebalance automatically.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The no-JavaScript colour-math claim is now accurate because JavaScript no longer injects sibling index or count variables.

edge_cases.rationale: The page keeps a CSS.supports probe and a static-color fallback for browsers without tree-counting function support.

references.rationale: The page includes the mandatory ChromeStatus link and the CSS Values 5 tree-counting specification link.

design_system.rationale: The touched styles use project design tokens for text, fallback color, borders, and surfaces.

summary: The timeline chart now demonstrates CSS tree-counting functions directly: JavaScript only changes the task list and schedule positions, while sibling-index() and sibling-count() drive the live color distribution in CSS.
